author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
jīvera bhāgye avanī vihare dona bhāi
bhuvana-mohana gorācā̐da āra nitāi

kaliyuge jīva jata chila acetana
hari-nāmāmṛta diyā karilā cetana

hena avatāra bhāi, kabhu śuni nāi
pātakī uddhāra kailā ghare ghare jāi

hena avatāra bhāi nāhi kona juge
kon avatāre bala pāpīra pāpa māge

rudhira paḍila aṅge khāiyā prahāra
jāci prema diyā tā’re karilā uddhāra

nāma⋅prema⋅sudhāte bharila tribhuvana
ekelā vañcita bhela e dāsa locana
en_translation:
By the good fortune of all living entities, the two brothers have appeared on this earth: the moon-like Gaura and Nitāi, who enchant the entire universe.

In this Age of Kali, they have awakened all the unconscious souls by giving them the nectar of the Holy Name of Lord Hari.

O brother! I have never heard of such an incarnation! They delivered all the sinful persons by going from house to house.

Such an incarnation has never appeared in any other age. Tell me, which incarnation ever begged the sinners to give up their sins?

Although they were beaten until blood flowed from their bodies, they still gave divine love and delivered those who struck them.

The three worlds were filled with the nectar of the Holy Name and divine love. Only this servant Locana remains deprived.
